name: Curse of the Crimson Throne — Ch.1: Edge of Anarchy (pilot)
caption: Phase-2 pilot: the Old Fishery (the hunt for Gaedren Lamm). Imports the chapter journal, the Old Fishery scene (map-note pins + staged tokens), the converted NPCs/hazards, and the treasure — organized into folders. Supply your own maps (Racooze's free CotCT battlemaps).
description: Phase-2 pilot: the Old Fishery (the hunt for Gaedren Lamm). Imports the chapter journal, the Old Fishery scene (map-note pins + staged tokens), the converted NPCs/hazards, and the treasure — organized into folders. Supply your own maps (Racooze's free CotCT battlemaps).

text.content:
A harrow card finds its way to each of you — and a hand-inked message naming a man you each have cause to hate: Gaedren Lamm. The fortune-teller Zellara knows where he dens, and asks you to end him.

Zellara's reading (see @UUID[.BmnAH8nXFFbtpHdU]{Haunted Fortunes}) draws the party together and points them at the Old Fishery, where crime-lord Gaedren Lamm hides with his thugs, his pet crocodile @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.2rMLYkUR47ZCQMUg]{Gobblegut}, and a workforce of enslaved orphans. In his den the PCs recover @UUID[Item.iNQaVcbkSM0VPXsa]{Queen Ileosa's stolen brooch}, @UUID[Item.I7HQNtjI9yPvQ4fA]{Gaedren's coded ledger}, and @UUID[Item.DPDen8gco6rznX9Y]{Zellara's haunted harrow deck} — and as they step back into the street, the king dies and Korvosa erupts into the riots of A City Gone Mad.

Advancement: the party should reach 2nd level after dealing with Gaedren. Milestone leveling (CHG-0007).

Party: 4 PCs, level 1 → 2. System: PF2e Remaster (Foundry v14 / pf2e 8.2.0).

Run it like Gotham. Tell players up front that Gaedren is a connector, not a campaign villain — this defuses the classic 'Lamm is a Lamb' let-down. By day 9 orphans toil, the thugs patrol, and the A7 doors stand open; by night every door locks, 26 orphans sleep in @UUID[.e6d3LqdDmDo97pVA]{A8}, and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.KHTYbQgR5hnFZdGL]{Bloo} prowls as a living alarm. The orphans are non-combatants — rescuing them is the heart of the dungeon.

The twist the players don't have yet: Zellara is already dead. Gaedren murdered her and fed her to Gobblegut; her spirit haunts her stolen deck. The PCs only learn this when they find her head in @UUID[.VCUD5FxhhchRv4fr]{A14} — so play her in the intro as a warm, desperate ally, not a mystery.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, Part 1: Haunted Fortunes — p.14-16.

It arrives where only you would find it — tucked in a spellbook, an altar, a stranger's pocket, the bottom of your tankard: a single harrow card, your card, with a few lines of bold ink on the back. It names Gaedren Lamm, and an address.

Each PC receives a harrow card matched to their highest ability + outlook, bearing an identical summons (Handout 1-1): come to 3 Lancet Street at sunset. Asking around, or a @Check[type:society|dc:12], identifies the address as the home of the Varisian fortune-teller Zellara Esmeranda.

At her home the PCs meet, compare cards, and Zellara tells her tale: Gaedren's pickpockets stole her heirloom harrow deck; her son Eran recovered it and was murdered for it; the Guard did nothing. She has tracked Gaedren to the Old Fishery at Westpier 17 and asks the PCs to bring him to justice. She then performs a harrowing to prepare them.

First Harrowing (CHG-0010). Run the reading as foreshadowing — a coming time of unrest and violence, and that the PCs are fated to become heroes of Korvosa. Mechanically, grant each PC a small pool of hero-point-style suit boons drawn from @UUID[Item.DPDen8gco6rznX9Y]{Zellara's deck}. This is the campaign's recurring subsystem; it returns at the start of every chapter.

Zellara is a haunt, not a living woman — her presence is split between this home and her stolen deck in Gaedren's lair, and her food, furnishings, and the welcome note are half-illusion (@Check[type:will|dc:18] to see through; success only makes them ghostly, not absent). If players guess early, roll with it — her purpose is simply to unite the party and aim them at Gaedren. After the fishery, a return visit finds the home long-abandoned and dust-choked; thereafter she manifests only through the deck.

No maps are shipped (copyright). Open the @UUID[Scene.PuUGEVunRqjIWFOj]{Old Fishery scene} — its map-note pins already link to each area page below, and its tokens reference the converted actors.

Drop in a battlemap (Racooze's free CotCT set, or your own at assets/maps/01-old-fishery.webp), then nudge the pins and tokens onto it — positions are staged in a grid for now (NEEDS GM REVIEW); the links are already correct. Drag @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.2rMLYkUR47ZCQMUg]{Crocodile} (rename 'Gobblegut') into @UUID[.zXnbWKFw5vnkEuW5]{A13} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.KHTYbQgR5hnFZdGL]{Guard Dog} ('Bloo') into @UUID[.UNkzTXihIylWOZcn]{A4}/@UUID[.e6d3LqdDmDo97pVA]{A8} from the pf2e Monster Core compendium.

The fishery is two map levels — an upper floor (A1–A9) and the waterline understructure (A10–A14). When you add the real map, split this into two scenes and re-pin; the area pages are already ordered for that split.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A. Old Fishery — p.17.

A creaking, mold-mottled building of boarded windows and solid old doors, perched on pilings over the Jeggare River. It still renders 'dock-dumpling' slurry from spoiled fish — a cover for the crime-lord who dens beneath it.

The fishery sits on a 13-ft embankment (@Check[type:athletics|dc:15] to climb the slick bank). Doors are hardness 5 / 10 HP (@Check[type:thievery|dc:15] to pick, or @Check[type:athletics|dc:15] to force); Yargin's brass key opens them all. Standing hazards: the @UUID[Actor.6y55S5DpXqnF40WL]{Slippery Boardwalk} (@UUID[.nbtuy4wuu5ATXEh1]{A3}) and the @UUID[Actor.xMhIQZS6GokTsulZ]{Rotten Ship Deck} (@UUID[.Obqhi4FG6wQ0IDZv]{A9}) — either can dump a PC into @UUID[Actor.lsdWSvGJ81hDy4it]{the shark's} water (@UUID[.yz5Ao0tTfBxREHWy]{A12}).

Day vs. night. The encounters assume a daytime raid (9 orphans working, thugs at their posts). At night every door is locked, Yargin sleeps in @UUID[.AdEtRIsLdlKOnWCQ]{A5}, Giggles and Hookshanks run roving patrols (≈9pm / midnight / 3am), and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.KHTYbQgR5hnFZdGL]{Bloo} wanders @UUID[.e6d3LqdDmDo97pVA]{A8} — his bark wakes the whole building.

The orphans ('Lamm's Lambs'). 9 work the fishery by day; all 26 sleep in @UUID[.e6d3LqdDmDo97pVA]{A8} at night. Rally them with @Check[type:diplomacy|dc:15] (Intimidation fails — they're inured to it); won over, they feed information or lend a hand. They know nothing past @UUID[.Obqhi4FG6wQ0IDZv]{A9} — those sent below never return.

They are non-combatants and never an XP-for-kill source (CHG-0107). Invaded at night they're too frightened to fight and flee into the slums once the thugs fall. Treat a rescued orphan as a 'Missing Child' background payoff, not a stat block.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A1 — p.18.

Weathered double doors stand shut in the fishery's flank, a broken signboard swinging from a length of rusted chain above them. Brine and the stink of week-dead fish hang thick in the air.

The main doors are locked (@Check[type:thievery|dc:15]). Most business runs through @UUID[.vejNR2kRU9hTjF7z]{A7}, so a knock — or a noisy attempt on the lock — brings @UUID[Actor.7uhbgkK2IOZOlJb3]{Yargin} up from @UUID[.HP8keHwIIY5gsNtZ]{A6} to answer.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A2 — p.18.

A fifteen-foot loading dock juts from the building. Carts wait half-laden with tar-caked barrels, each daubed with a red, fish-shaped splotch. A rickety stair drops to a second door barely three feet above the river.

By day the doors into @UUID[.vejNR2kRU9hTjF7z]{A7} stand open for the daily slurry shipment; the door to @UUID[.e6d3LqdDmDo97pVA]{A8} is always locked (@Check[type:thievery|dc:15]). The orphans do the heavy hauling under Hookshanks' eye.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A3 — p.18-20.

A slippery boardwalk clings to the south wall on barnacle-eaten pilings, worn thin below the waterline. It runs about thirteen feet above the river, sloping down toward the derelict ship to the east.

The @UUID[Actor.6y55S5DpXqnF40WL]{Slippery Boardwalk} hazard: a careful pace is safe, but moving fast or fighting on it risks a fall into @UUID[Actor.lsdWSvGJ81hDy4it]{the shark's} water (@UUID[.yz5Ao0tTfBxREHWy]{A12}); the planks also groan and give under heavy loads. The door into @UUID[.HP8keHwIIY5gsNtZ]{A6} is locked.

Despite its state, this boardwalk is Gaedren's own private way in and out of his den (@UUID[.zXnbWKFw5vnkEuW5]{A13}) — he uses it only a few times a month, spending days or weeks below to avoid being seen.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A4 — p.19.

A single desk and a moldering chair stand in the middle of this disused room. A nest of ratty furs and straw is heaped beneath the desk.

In theory Yargin meets new customers here — a rarity. Any real noise in this room quickly brings both @UUID[Actor.7uhbgkK2IOZOlJb3]{Yargin} and @UUID[Actor.qH96VshE6CZqfajv]{Hookshanks} to investigate.

  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.KHTYbQgR5hnFZdGL]{Bloo — Guard Dog (−1)}
BlooTrivial · 20 XP

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.KHTYbQgR5hnFZdGL]{Bloo}, Yargin's foul-tempered cur, sleeps under the desk and attacks any unfamiliar scent on sight. While he lives, a thug gets a circumstance bonus to bully the orphans into fighting.

Silence him fast — @Check[type:stealth|dc:15] to slip past, @Check[type:nature|dc:15] to calm, or a quick kill — before the barking rouses the fishery.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A5 — p.20.

Two sets of bunks flank a boarded-over window. Three are slept-in; the fourth stands bare.

The thugs — Yargin, Hookshanks, and Giggles — share this room. They distrust one another and keep nothing of value here.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A6 — p.19-21.

A desk wedged into one corner — its bulk blocking the western door — overflows with chalk-scrawled slate boards. A slouching cabinet leans against the east wall.

The fishery's 'books': slates of transactions and addresses, compiled monthly into scrolls in the cabinet — cover paperwork for any Guard inspection. @UUID[Actor.7uhbgkK2IOZOlJb3]{Yargin Balko} works here and carries the brass key.

A floorboard behind the chair is a hidden, barred trapdoor (@Check[type:perception|dc:18] to spot; @Check[type:athletics|dc:17] or break to force) dropping straight into the boss's chamber (@UUID[.zXnbWKFw5vnkEuW5]{A13}). A meal-pulley rig makes the climb down trivial — a flanking route onto Gaedren that skips the whole lower floor.

  • @UUID[Actor.7uhbgkK2IOZOlJb3]{Yargin Balko (1)}
Yargin BalkoLow · 40 XP

Opens with thrown Acid Flasks, then falls back to his crossbow; he's terrified of melee.

Morale: at the first melee hit (or once his acid is spent) Yargin panics and flees down the trapdoor to warn Gaedren — and may lead the PCs right onto the boss. If he reaches the den, start the @UUID[.zXnbWKFw5vnkEuW5]{A13} fight with Gobblegut already enraged.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A7 — p.20-22.

The reek of fish and sweat stings the eyes. A great trough of half-rancid fish and brine drains through wooden chutes into a larger room beyond; a desk and a tall cabinet sit opposite.

Four orphans feed the chutes here. The floor around the trough is slick (@Check[type:acrobatics|dc:15] if moving faster than a Step). The cabinet holds petty cash, locked (@Check[type:thievery|dc:15]; Yargin's key).

@UUID[Actor.qH96VshE6CZqfajv]{Hookshanks Gruller}, a gnome taskmaster, oversees the work — and dresses as one of the orphans, passing for one until a PC beats his Deception with @Check[type:perception|dc:17] (gnome PCs get a bonus).

  • @UUID[Actor.qH96VshE6CZqfajv]{Hookshanks Gruller (1)}
  • 4× @UUID[Actor.UQr3ApF78GW7eghw]{Lamm's Lamb} (non-combatant)
Hookshanks + 4 orphansLow · 40 XP

Hookshanks orders the orphans to attack, then opens the @UUID[.UNkzTXihIylWOZcn]{A4} door to loose @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.KHTYbQgR5hnFZdGL]{Bloo} and raise the alarm; he fights with a kukri.

Rally the kids (@Check[type:diplomacy|dc:15]) and an older boy, Kester, blinds Hookshanks with a faceful of rancid fish. Hookshanks surrenders at low HP and tells all — except what lies in the den (@UUID[.VCUD5FxhhchRv4fr]{A14}).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A8 — p.21-22.

The main floor is slick with river water, weed, and fish blood. Catwalks ring an eight-foot tar-caulked slurry vat; a wide hole in the south floor opens straight onto the river below. Rows of small hammocks hang beneath the walks.

The heart of the operation. @UUID[Actor.mHUhxeaalWkTU1h4]{Giggles}, a half-orc brute, oversees 5 orphans by day; all 26 sleep here at night. The hole drops to the river and @UUID[Actor.lsdWSvGJ81hDy4it]{the jigsaw shark} — the thugs toss scraps through it to keep the shark close and the children terrified.

  • @UUID[Actor.mHUhxeaalWkTU1h4]{Giggles (1)}
  • 5× @UUID[Actor.UQr3ApF78GW7eghw]{orphans} (non-combatant)
Giggles [+ Bloo at night]Low · 40 XP (60 with Bloo)

Giggles uses Bludgeoner to capture PCs nonlethally for Gaedren, switching to lethal below half HP and quaffing healing potions; then he fights to the death (Orc Ferocity).

Night raid: this floor is wall-to-wall sleeping children — a fight here endangers them. The river-hole is also the quiet route down to the underpier (@UUID[.yz5Ao0tTfBxREHWy]{A12}) and the den, bypassing the front rooms entirely.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A9 — p.23-24.

A derelict ship lies lashed to the pilings by layers of rotting rope, its hull furred with weed and barnacles. A narrow walkway runs along the starboard rail to an aft-cabin door marked with a daubed red fish.

No longer seaworthy — Gaedren's route to the den. The @UUID[Actor.xMhIQZS6GokTsulZ]{Rotten Ship Deck} hazard: everything but the stern is rotten, and a Medium+ creature moving toward the bow crashes through into the hold (@UUID[.hZUJ2WpPj9SJqTVg]{A11}). Stairs in the cabin lead down to the spider nest.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A10 — p.24.

The air is thick and musty. Sheets of cobweb drape the walls; mounds of blanket, cushion, and straw clutter the floor. A narrow stair drops into the ship's hold.

The cabin is the lair of a single cat-sized @UUID[Actor.1M94tm3wnSRGcTG5]{drain spider} that lunges at the first creature through the door.

  • @UUID[Actor.1M94tm3wnSRGcTG5]{Drain Spider (−1)}
Drain SpiderTrivial · 20 XP

One drain spider, off-guard against the surprised intruder it lunges at.

The spiders can't abide the vermin repellent looted from @UUID[Item.ymuRwMg6WfWIwkne]{Gaedren's coffer} (@UUID[.zXnbWKFw5vnkEuW5]{A13}) — a coated creature auto-repels them.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A11 — p.24.

Dark and dank, the hold smells of mildew. Crates and barrels lie stacked among puddles of standing river water.

Four more @UUID[Actor.1M94tm3wnSRGcTG5]{drain spiders} infest the hold.

Gaedren built a secret door into the hull (@Check[type:perception|dc:17]) opening to the underpier (@UUID[.yz5Ao0tTfBxREHWy]{A12}) and the boss. Spotting his tracks in the filth (@Check[type:survival|dc:15]) grants a circumstance bonus to find it.

  • 4× @UUID[Actor.1M94tm3wnSRGcTG5]{Drain Spider (−1)}
Drain Spider nestModerate · 80 XP (trivial with repellent)

Four drain spiders swarm anything that enters. Pair this with the @UUID[Actor.xMhIQZS6GokTsulZ]{Rotten Deck} collapse from @UUID[.Obqhi4FG6wQ0IDZv]{A9} for a single nasty moment.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A12 — p.24-25.

A narrow space runs beneath the fishery, three feet of headroom above the foamy river. Moss and rusted chain hang between the pilings, and a floating walkway threads west to a tiny two-and-a-half-foot door.

Gaedren's escape skiffs are tied along the walkway. The squat door (@Check[type:thievery|dc:17]) leads into the den (@UUID[.zXnbWKFw5vnkEuW5]{A13}); a Medium creature must stoop to squeeze through.

  • @UUID[Actor.lsdWSvGJ81hDy4it]{Jigsaw Shark (1)}
Jigsaw SharkLow · 40 XP · avoidable

The shark scavenges scraps drifting from above. It attacks anything that falls into the water, but only leaps onto the walkway if it is first attacked and damaged.

Stay dry and it stays a hazard, not a fight — though the @UUID[Actor.6y55S5DpXqnF40WL]{boardwalk} and @UUID[Actor.xMhIQZS6GokTsulZ]{rotten deck} above exist precisely to feed it victims.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A13 — p.24-26.

A chill chamber opens over a pit of black river water, crossed by two five-foot walkways. Rusted manacles dangle from mossy ropes above the pool. On the far side, cabinets and lockboxes spill dingy 'treasures' across three cluttered tables. Something heavy shifts in the water below.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.2rMLYkUR47ZCQMUg]{Gobblegut} lurks in the pit; @UUID[Actor.RKfT6vJ5guinSBjo]{Gaedren Lamm} sorts the day's haul at his tables across it. Reaching him means crossing the gator's water.

Those manacles are where Gaedren feeds doomed orphans to Gobblegut for sport — the PCs may arrive mid-'feeding,' a child dangling over the snapping jaws. Anyone who names Zellara earns a leering reply that 'she's in the next room' — her head waits in @UUID[.VCUD5FxhhchRv4fr]{A14}. Tactics: Gaedren opens with Spur the Beast to enrage Gobblegut, snipes from across the pool, Nimble Dodges focus fire, and flees to the @UUID[.yz5Ao0tTfBxREHWy]{A12} skiffs at ≤8 HP — though the abused gator may take him first.

  • @UUID[Actor.RKfT6vJ5guinSBjo]{Gaedren Lamm (2)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.2rMLYkUR47ZCQMUg]{Gobblegut — Crocodile (2)}
BOSS — Gaedren + GobblegutSEVERE · 120 XP @ level 1 (Moderate @ level 2)

The crocodile is the real threat; Gaedren is a frail catalyst who weaponizes it and snipes from cover.

Scaling: 3 PCs → Weak Crocodile · 5–6 PCs → Elite Crocodile and/or pull a drain spider from the pool · if Yargin reached Gaedren first, Gobblegut starts pre-enraged.

On the tables: @UUID[Item.ymuRwMg6WfWIwkne]{a darkwood coffer — 20 doses of shiver + 7 applications of vermin repellent}.

⚠ Track what the PCs do with the body. Left here, Gaedren's son Rolth animates it — the PCs meet an undead Gaedren in the Dead Warrens at the chapter's end. See @UUID[.tkc0z28kYBKNBgHd]{Conversion Notes}.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Ch.1, A14 — p.26-28.

A squalid bedroom-study: a lumpy bed against one wall, a table heaped with rotting food and scuttling roaches, a sagging dresser. At the foot of the bed sits a locked strongbox, a moldy ledger resting on its lid. A fly-blown hatbox sits atop the dresser.

Gaedren's private quarters. The strongbox opens to the rusty iron key he carries, or @Check[type:thievery|dc:15].

The reveal. The hatbox holds Zellara's severed head, crudely made up to mimic life (@Check[type:medicine|dc:14]: dead for weeks) — confirming the woman who hired the party has been dead all along. Beneath it lies @UUID[Item.DPDen8gco6rznX9Y]{her haunted harrow deck}, still inhabited by her spirit. Atop the strongbox is @UUID[Item.I7HQNtjI9yPvQ4fA]{Gaedren's coded ledger}; inside, @UUID[Item.iNQaVcbkSM0VPXsa]{Queen Ileosa's Brooch} and the hoard. A key-shaped masterwork dagger among the loot is a gift from Gaedren's estranged son Rolth — foreshadowing the next chapter (@Check[type:society|dc:20] recognizes the killer's signature blade).

Full parcel on the @UUID[.zceSTiompH1bHFDc]{Treasure} page.

Click any creature to open its sheet. Build rationale: reports/npc_monster_conversion_report.md.

  • @UUID[Actor.RKfT6vJ5guinSBjo]{Gaedren Lamm} (L2) — @UUID[.zXnbWKFw5vnkEuW5]{A13} boss · @UUID[Actor.7uhbgkK2IOZOlJb3]{Yargin Balko} (L1) — @UUID[.HP8keHwIIY5gsNtZ]{A6} · @UUID[Actor.qH96VshE6CZqfajv]{Hookshanks Gruller} (L1) — @UUID[.vejNR2kRU9hTjF7z]{A7} · @UUID[Actor.mHUhxeaalWkTU1h4]{Giggles} (L1) — @UUID[.e6d3LqdDmDo97pVA]{A8}
  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.2rMLYkUR47ZCQMUg]{Gobblegut} = Crocodile (L2) · @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.KHTYbQgR5hnFZdGL]{Bloo} = Guard Dog (L−1) · @UUID[Actor.1M94tm3wnSRGcTG5]{Drain Spider} (L−1) · @UUID[Actor.lsdWSvGJ81hDy4it]{Jigsaw Shark} (L1) · @UUID[Actor.UQr3ApF78GW7eghw]{Lamm's Lambs} (non-combatant)
  • Zellara — the harrow-spirit guide, bound to @UUID[Item.DPDen8gco6rznX9Y]{her deck} (story NPC, no stat block).

Rebuilt to PF2e level 1→2 (the PF1e gp hoard is discarded; CHG-0008). Full table: reports/treasure_report.md.

  • @UUID[Item.b04PLWVkmm3RM4nm]{+1 Dagger (Gaedren's)} — boss loot, a fitting first permanent magic weapon · +1 armor potency (his padded armor) · A14 hoard → a 1st-rank scroll + ~50 gp gems.
  • @UUID[Item.dK4hnpH7s67RYnNI]{Garnet amulet} (~8 gp) · @UUID[Item.ymuRwMg6WfWIwkne]{Coffer + vermin repellent} (~12 gp) · 2× Acid Flask + Thunderstone (Yargin) · 3× minor Healing Potion (Giggles) · ~30 gp coin.
  • @UUID[Item.I7HQNtjI9yPvQ4fA]{Coded ledger} → Korvosan Guard bounty 15 gp (30 gp with the cipher key).

Story items — do not let players cash these out for level-breaking gold. @UUID[Item.iNQaVcbkSM0VPXsa]{Queen Ileosa's Brooch} launches the queen's audience and the chapter's main reward; @UUID[Item.DPDen8gco6rznX9Y]{Zellara's Harrow Deck} is the Harrow subsystem anchor (CHG-0010). The original PF1e parcel (a 2,000 gp brooch, gold ingot, etc.) is deliberately not restored.

Mirrors conversion_change_log.md (CHG-0101…0108). Story, villain, flow, and the closing cliffhanger are all preserved; nothing here is an approval-gated change.

  • Gaedren rebuilt as a credible Severe, with Gobblegut as the real threat — fixes the 'Lamm is a Lamb' anticlimax and PF2e solo-boss math. Added Spur the Beast / Nimble Dodge; kept Limping Gait. ⚠ Confirm Severe @L1 vs running it @L2.
  • Gobblegut & Bloo referenced as the official Crocodile / Guard Dog (no rebuild).
  • DCs re-derived to PF2e level-based / simple values (the PF1e locked-door spam collapsed to a single 'pick = 15' line).
  • Treasure rebuilt to PF2e level 1→2 (CHG-0008).
  • Drain Spider Venom: PF1e Con-drain → PF2e 1-stage poison + enfeebled.
  • Harrowing → hero-point-style suit pool (CHG-0010).
  • Orphans = explicit non-combatants (Rally / Cower); never an XP-for-kill source (CHG-0107).
  • Forward hooks: leaving Gaedren's body → undead Gaedren in the Dead Warrens; the Brooch → the queen's audience; the deck → recurring Harrowings.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)
Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value: +1d6 precision damage vs off-guard creatures (already in his Strikes when applicable).

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value: An old leg wound caps his Speed at 15 ft; he snipes from across the gator pool rather than kiting.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value:
Trigger A creature he can see attacks him.

Effect +2 circumstance bonus to AC vs that attack.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value: Gaedren makes a Hand Crossbow Strike against Gobblegut; on a hit the enraged crocodile gains a +2 status bonus to attack/damage and must Strike the nearest creature other than Gaedren next turn. His signature: turn his pet into a weapon, then snipe from cover.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)
Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

details.blurb: Decrepit crime lord; first villain of the AP
details.publicNotes: Role: the AP's first villain and every PC's revenge payoff — but a frail catalyst, not a tank. The threat is the crocodile; Gaedren weaponizes it. Tactics: R1 Spur the Beast, then snipe across the pool; Nimble Dodge when focus-fired; flees to the A12 skiffs at ≤8 HP. If killed and left in A14, his son Rolth animates the body in the Dead Warrens (D13) — track it.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value: Draw and throw an Acid Flask in one action (carries 3).

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)
Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

details.publicNotes: Role: Gaedren's fence; answers the front door, fights from A6. Opens with thrown Acid Flasks, falls back to crossbow. Flees down the hidden trapdoor to warn Gaedren if losing. Carries the brass key (all fishery doors).

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)
Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)
Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value: +1d6 precision vs off-guard.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value: Passes as one of Lamm's Lambs; @Check[type:perception|dc:17] vs his Deception to spot him before he Sneak Attacks.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value: At <half HP he talks; knows the fishery and Gaedren's routine except the den (A14).

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)
Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

details.blurb: Gnome taskmaster who hides among the orphans
details.publicNotes: Role: taskmaster over the orphans in A7; hides among them; can surrender and inform.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)
Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)
Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)
Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value: Deals nonlethal with his Flail at no penalty; +1 circumstance to attack on nonlethal Strikes vs foes above half HP.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value:
Trigger reduced to 0 HP.

Stays at 1 HP, wounded 1. Once per day.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)
Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

details.publicNotes: Role: muscle on the main floor (A8); tries to capture PCs nonlethally for Gaedren, then fights to the death. Carries 3 minor healing potions.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)
Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value: Saving Throw @Check[type:fortitude|dc:15]; Stage 1 1d4 poison + enfeebled 1 (1 round). A spider that smells Gaedren's vermin repellent (looted in A13) is sickened and won't approach a coated creature.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value: Attacks the first creature to enter its lair, off-guard to the surprised target.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

details.publicNotes: Cat-sized vermin nesting in the wreck (A10 = 1; A11 = 4). Trivially repelled by vermin repellent.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)
Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value: While hidden in water, Swim up to Speed and Strike; target off-guard.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value: Attacks anything that falls in the water, but only leaps onto the underpier (A12) if attacked and damaged first.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

details.publicNotes: Scavenger under the fishery. Avoidable if the PCs stay dry; the hazards feed creatures to it.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)
Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value: An orphan that takes damage flees to A8, or out of the fishery if the thugs are down. Orphans invaded at night are too frightened to fight.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value: A PC can rally an orphan (@Check[type:diplomacy|dc:15]; Intimidation fails) to Aid or reveal info. Non-combatants — not an XP source for killing. Rescuing them is the dungeon's heart (a 'Missing Child' background payoff).

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

details.publicNotes: Enslaved child pickpockets: 9 by day across A7/A8, 26 asleep in A8 at night. The moral core of the dungeon.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value:
Trigger A creature Strides >5 ft or fights on it.

Effect @Check[type:reflex|dc:15|basic:true] or fall prone; on a critical failure, slide off and fall 13 ft into the river (@Damage[1[bludgeoning]]) and the Jigsaw Shark's water (A12).

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

details.description: A barnacle-slick boardwalk (A3) 13 ft above shark water; the planks also groan under heavy loads.
details.disable: @Check[type:acrobatics|dc:15] to cross carefully, or brace a stretch (@Check[type:crafting|dc:15]).

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value:
Trigger A Medium+ creature moves onto the foredeck toward the bow.

Effect @Check[type:reflex|dc:16|basic:true] or crash to the hold (A11) for @Damage[1d6[bludgeoning]] and land prone among the drain spiders.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

stealth.details: @Check[type:perception|dc:16] to spot the unsafe foredeck first.

details.description: The derelict Kraken's Folly (A9) has a rotten foredeck; only the stern holds weight.
details.disable: @Check[type:crafting|dc:16] to shore up a path, or keep to the sound stern.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value: Story item. A gold filigree house-drake brooch (broken clasp) from Gaedren's strongbox (A14); Queen Ileosa's. Returning it earns an audience with the queen and launches the rest of Chapter 1. Loot value trivial; narrative value high — don't let players sell it for level-breaking gold.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value: Story item — the Harrow subsystem anchor (CHG-0010). Inhabited by the spirit of the murdered fortune-teller Zellara; recovered in A14. Anchors the recurring Harrowing (hero-point-style suit boons). Becomes the Harrow Deck of Many Things in Ch.6.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value: Quest item. Records Gaedren's crimes in a Varisian cipher. @Check[type:society|dc:17] (must read Varisian) to decode. Guard bounty: 15 gp (30 gp with the cipher key). Unlocks the 'Framed' background payoff.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value: A cheap garnet amulet (Yargin). Converted treasure: ~8 gp gem (PF1e 100 gp, re-scaled).

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value: A darkwood coffer (~10 gp) from A13 holding 20 doses of shiver and 7 applications of vermin repellent (drives off the A10/A11 drain spiders). Shiver is a story drug, not loot; destroying it pays off a 'Drug Addict' background hook.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

description.value: Gaedren's prized magical dagger — the boss's signature loot and a fitting first permanent magic weapon for a level 1-2 party. (His PF1e '+1 padded armor' similarly converts to a minor armor reward — see the Treasure page.)

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)
